message:
fix(sync): add PolledAfterCompletion guards to all sync primitive futures

Futures for Mutex, RwLock, and Semaphore could previously be polled
again after returning Ready, which violates the Future contract and
can cause subtle double-acquisition or use-after-completion bugs.

Each future type now tracks a `completed` flag that is set on any
terminal Poll::Ready return. Subsequent polls return a dedicated
`PolledAfterCompletion` error variant instead of silently re-entering
the acquisition path.

Additional changes in this commit:
- Mutex: add OwnedLockFuture for Arc<Mutex<T>> usage patterns where
  the lock must outlive the borrow scope
- OnceCell: extract transition_out_of_initializing() to deduplicate
  the state→INITIALIZED + wake_all + cvar.notify_all sequence across
  three call sites; add BlockingWaitHook test infrastructure for
  deterministic concurrency testing
- New test: watch_lost_wakeup.rs exercises the watch channel to verify
  waker registration survives dropped-then-recreated futures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
